claimText: KAVACH is an indigenously developed Automatic Train Protection system.
evidenceExcerpt: Indigenously Automatic Train Protection system designed to improve train safety and efficiency.
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and purpose of KAVACH.
distractorHooks: Developed in collaboration with foreign entities like Japan or France
It is a manual alert system without automatic protection

claimText: The KAVACH system automatically applies the train's brakes if the loco pilot fails to do so.
evidenceExcerpt: automatically applying the brakes if the pilot fails to do so.
examUse: Statement-based question on the functional capabilities of KAVACH.
distractorHooks: Only provides audio-visual warnings without intervening in braking
Completely replaces the loco pilot for autonomous driving

claimText: KAVACH utilizes Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tags placed along the track to track train positions.
evidenceExcerpt: Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tags are placed throughout the entire track length to enable the Kavach to track train positions
examUse: Statement-based question on the underlying technology of KAVACH.
distractorHooks: Relies exclusively on GPS satellite tracking
Uses optical cameras for track monitoring

claimText: The KAVACH system can operate smoothly in extreme weather conditions such as fog.
evidenceExcerpt: Operates smoothly in extreme weather conditions like fog.
examUse: Statement-based question on the operational limitations and capabilities of KAVACH.
distractorHooks: System becomes non-functional during heavy fog or low visibility
Requires clear line-of-sight to operate effectively
